Claim Integrity.question: What is Claim Integrity?
Claim Integrity.answer: Claim Integrity is the practice of making a healthcare claim correct before it is ever created, not catching errors after it is submitted. A claim carries the diagnosis, the services performed, and the codes a payer uses to decide payment. Most of what determines whether a claim gets paid is set before the claim is built, in patient access, documentation, and charge capture. Claim Integrity moves the check to that point. The claim is verified against payer rules at the moment it forms, so it leaves the building clean.

Claim Integrity.question: How is Claim Integrity different from claim scrubbing?
Claim Integrity.answer: Claim scrubbing checks a claim after it has been built. It looks for formatting errors and missing fields right before the claim goes to the clearinghouse. Claim Integrity works earlier. It validates the documentation and coding logic before the claim exists, which is where most denials are actually created. Scrubbing catches typos. Claim Integrity prevents the claim from being wrong in the first place.

Claim Integrity.question: Why do most claim denials happen before billing ever sees the claim?
Claim Integrity.answer: A denial is a payer's refusal to pay a submitted claim. By the time billing receives a claim, the work that caused a denial has usually already happened. The cause sits upstream: patient access, clinical documentation, and charge capture. It is a missing prior authorization, a documentation gap, a charge that was never recorded, or a code that does not match payer-specific rules. Billing inherits these problems. It does not create them.

Claim Integrity.question: What is the difference between pre-claim and post-claim revenue cycle work?
Claim Integrity.answer: Post-claim work happens after a claim is submitted. It includes denial appeals, rework, and follow-up. It is reactive and expensive. Pre-claim work happens before submission. It catches problems while they are still cheap to fix. Grelin operates at the pre-claim stage.

The Grelin Platform.question: What is Grelin Health?
The Grelin Platform.answer: Grelin Health is a healthcare software company. It builds the intelligence layer for Claim Integrity. Grelin sits above clearinghouse infrastructure and checks claims against payer rules before they are submitted. The result is fewer denials, less rework, and faster payment.

The Grelin Platform.question: What does the Grelin platform do?
The Grelin Platform.answer: The Grelin platform reviews a healthcare claim before it is submitted and confirms it will hold up against payer rules. It reads the documentation and coding behind a claim, applies payer-specific logic, and flags anything that would cause a denial. The correction happens before the claim leaves the practice.

The Grelin Platform.question: Is Grelin a clearinghouse?
The Grelin Platform.answer: No. A clearinghouse is the infrastructure that transmits claims from a provider to a payer. It moves claims. It does not judge whether they are correct. Grelin is the intelligence layer above that infrastructure. It decides whether a claim is right before the clearinghouse ever sends it. Grelin works with your clearinghouse. It does not replace it.

The Grelin Platform.question: How does Grelin work with our existing clearinghouse and billing system?
The Grelin Platform.answer: Grelin sits in front of the claim submission step. Your clearinghouse, EHR, and practice management system stay in place. Grelin reviews the claim before it reaches the clearinghouse. Clean claims pass through. Problem claims get flagged with the specific reason and the fix.

The Grelin Platform.question: What does it mean that Grelin is the intelligence layer above the clearinghouse?
The Grelin Platform.answer: The clearinghouse is the infrastructure layer that transmits claims between providers and payers. The intelligence layer sits above that. It decides whether a claim is correct before the infrastructure sends it. Grelin is that intelligence layer. The clearinghouse moves the claim. Grelin makes sure the claim is right.

Results and ROI.question: How does Grelin reduce claim denials?
Results and ROI.answer: Grelin catches the cause of a denial before the claim is created. Most denials come from missing authorizations, documentation gaps, and coding that does not match payer rules. Grelin checks all of that at the pre-claim stage and flags the issue while it is still easy to fix. Fewer broken claims leave the building. Fewer denials come back.

Results and ROI.question: What return can a practice expect from Grelin?
Results and ROI.answer: The return shows up in three places. First, less rework — staff stop spending hours correcting and resubmitting denied claims. Second, faster payment — clean claims get paid on the first submission. Third, fewer write-offs — claims that would have been abandoned get paid.

Results and ROI.question: Who uses Grelin?
Results and ROI.answer: Grelin is built for CFOs, VPs of revenue cycle, and MSO operators who feel the cost of denials directly and own the decision to fix the root cause.

Results and ROI.question: Does Grelin work for management services organizations?
Results and ROI.answer: Yes. An MSO runs billing and operations for multiple practices at once. Scale is where small claim errors get expensive. A documentation gap that costs one practice a little costs an MSO that same gap multiplied across every practice it manages. Grelin enforces consistent Claim Integrity across every practice an MSO supports.

Results and ROI.question: How long does Grelin take to implement?
Results and ROI.answer: Grelin connects in front of your existing claim submission process without replacing your clearinghouse, EHR, or practice management system. Because Grelin adds a layer rather than swapping out infrastructure, it is faster to adopt than a full system change.

Security, AI, and Integration.question: How does Grelin use artificial intelligence?
Security, AI, and Integration.answer: Grelin uses AI to apply payer rules and coding logic to a claim automatically. It removes manual claim-by-claim review, enforces correct coding logic before a claim is built, and prevents rework by catching problems early. The AI works in service of one outcome: claims that are correct before they are submitted.

Security, AI, and Integration.question: Does Grelin replace our billing team?
Security, AI, and Integration.answer: No. Grelin removes the lowest-value part of billing work — finding and fixing errors after the fact. Your billing team stops reworking broken claims and starts handling claims that are already clean.

Security, AI, and Integration.question: Is Grelin HIPAA compliant?
Security, AI, and Integration.answer: Grelin is built to operate in healthcare environments that handle protected health information and is designed to meet HIPAA requirements. Security and compliance practices are reviewed as part of any deployment.

Security, AI, and Integration.question: Can Grelin integrate with our electronic health record?
Security, AI, and Integration.answer: Grelin reads the documentation and charge information that flows out of your EHR and practice management system. Grelin reviews that information before the claim is finalized. Your existing systems stay in place.

Wound Care.question: Does implementing Claim Integrity require changing our EMR/EHR provider?
Wound Care.answer: No. Grelin is a lightweight intelligence layer. It integrates into your existing Epic, Cerner, or Athena workflows via standard HL7/FHIR APIs. Doctors and registrars work in their preferred systems; Grelin delivers warnings into their native interface.

Wound Care.question: What causes most wound care claim denials?
Wound Care.answer: Wound care denials are most often caused by incomplete documentation of wound type, size, depth, and treatment. A missing measurement or unsupported code level is enough to trigger a denial. Wound.ai checks documentation completeness before the claim is submitted.

Pain Management.question: Why are authorization gaps common in pain management billing?
Pain Management.answer: Pain management procedures often require prior authorization before service is rendered. The rules vary by payer, procedure code, and patient plan. A missing or expired authorization means the claim is already wrong before billing sees it. Pain.ai validates authorization status at the pre-claim stage.

Pain Management.question: What are the most common pain management claim denial reasons?
Pain Management.answer: The most common denial reasons are missing prior authorization, lack of medical necessity documentation, incorrect procedure codes, and frequency limit violations. Each originates before the claim is built. Catching them upstream prevents the rework cycle entirely.

MSO.question: How does Claim Integrity scale across an MSO?
MSO.answer: An MSO multiplies every billing error across its entire network. A single documentation pattern that causes denials at one practice causes the same denials at all practices. Grelin enforces consistent pre-claim validation rules across every site an MSO operates.

MSO.question: How can an MSO standardize revenue integrity across sites?
MSO.answer: Standardization requires applying the same validation rules to every claim regardless of which practice submits it. Grelin provides a single intelligence layer above all practice management systems in an MSO. Every claim passes through the same pre-submission checks before it reaches the clearinghouse.
